prose: Fukui reactivity indices for aniline were computed with DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied aniline.xyz, used as-is (not re-optimized at this level). Partial charges: Mulliken (PySCF). Three SCF calculations were run: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Density fitting was OFF (exact four-centre integrals). All SCF calculations converged. Fukui charge conservation: Σf⁺ = 1.0000 and Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (both pass). No engine warnings. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. Most nucleophilic atom: N (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.20452359. Most electrophilic atoms: C-ortho (atoms 3 & 4), f⁺ ≈ 2.2976 and 2.2966; C-para (atom 7), f⁺ = 1.4032. The headline value reported under 'value' is f⁻ of N (the most nucleophilic site). CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard aniline.xyz. Plot saved to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-172208_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
